Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Explodes on Launchpad
articles.summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded while undergoing a test on a launchpad in Florida on Thursday evening. The explosion is the latest blow to its reputation as a reliable alternative to SpaceX’s Falcon 9. (Source: @JConcilus via Associated Press)

articles.title: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes on Launchpad in Florida
articles.summary: User generated video verified by Associated Press shows the Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explode in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a Florida launchpad Thursday evening. The firm was preparing the vehicle for its fourth launch, which was slated to deploy a batch of satellites for Amazon.com Inc.’s Leo, a rival satellite network to SpaceX’s Starlink. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: The drone hit a residential building, injuring two people and causing a fire, officials said.

articles.title: Oil Declines on US-Iran Ceasefire Optimism
articles.summary: Oil fell as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days. Brent is set for its biggest monthly drop since 2020 on optimism that flows through the Strait of Hormuz may resume. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

Iran's Pezeshkian thanks Malaysia and Pakistan for support
articles.summary: Iran's Pezeshkian thanks Malaysia and Pakistan for support Following conversation with the prime ministers of Malaysia and Pakistan, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has thanked the two countries for their cooperation with Tehran.  "I thanked Malaysia for its humanitarian stance and Pakistan for its initiative and effective efforts to reach an agreement," Pezeshkian said in a post on X. "Iran's policy is to expand cooperation with Muslim and neighbouring countries in all fields," he added.

Report: US military planes at Ben Gurion Airport cost Israel $248m in losses
articles.summary: Report: US military planes at Ben Gurion Airport cost Israel $248m in losses The presence of US military planes at Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport has cost airport authorities 700 million shekels ($248m) in losses over the past two months, public broadcaster KAN reported. Quoting Sharon Kedmi, director general of the Israel Airports Authority, the report indicates that the airport is operating at just one-third of its capacity due to the presence of US military refueling aircrafts. "We are at the limit of our capabilities," Kedmi said, adding that around 70 percent of acti

Hong Kong: On Track to Meet or Exceed 2026 Visitor Target

articles.title: Boston Dynamics' Schulman on US Robotics Strategy
articles.summary: Brendan Schulman, Vice President of Policy and Government Relations at Boston Dynamics, discusses his view on how the US government and firms should approach the development of robotics technology, and the competition on deploying such technology in other countries like China and Japan. He speaks on the sidelines of the Tokyo Humanoids Summit on "Bloomberg: The China Show" (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Alpac Capital Agrees to Buy Adria News Network From United Group
articles.summary: Alpac Capital agreed to buy Adria News Network, which has news assets in Bosnia, Croatia, Montenegro, Serbia and Slovenia, from United Group BV, according to a statement released Friday.

articles.summary: Shanghai Zhengxin Food Group Co., a chain specializing in fried chicken cutlets, is exploring an initial public offering in Hong Kong that may raise about $300 million, people with knowledge of the matter said.

articles.title: Humanoids: from Spectacle to Scale | Bloomberg Tech: Asia 5/29/2026

(Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Honda R&D’s Yoshiike on Global Robotics Competition
articles.summary: Takahide Yoshiike, Executive Chief Engineer for Honda’s R&D Frontier Robotics division, notes that while global competition in robotics is intense, each region has distinct strengths. Chinese companies excel at reducing production costs for humanoid robots, the United States leads in AI chip development, and Japan’s advantage lies in manufacturing precise, high-quality hardware. He also discusses the evolution of Honda’s humanoid robotics efforts. He speaks with Shery Ahn from the sidelines of the Tokyo Humanoids Summit on "Bloomberg Tech Asia". (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: The incident is the latest setback for Jeff Bezos’s space venture as it seeks to narrow the gap with Elon Musk’s SpaceX.

articles.title: Google Deepmind's Parada on Robotics Technology
articles.summary: Carolina Parada, VP and Head of Robotics at Google Deepmind, says “embodied intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence.” She is focused on pushing the boundaries of robots’ ability to perform highly dexterous tasks, such as folding origami or packing a lunch box. Parada also discusses the development and research direction of Gemini Robotics. She speaks with Shery Ahn on the sidelines of the Tokyo Humanoids Summit on "Bloomberg Tech Asia". (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iron Ore Heads for Monthly Loss as Coal Spike Pressures Margins
articles.summary: Iron ore headed for a monthly loss as a short-lived rally triggered by a fatal steelmaking-coal mine accident in China’s Shanxi province faded, leaving investors focused on mill margins and mounting shipments.

articles.title: Vance says US and Iran are 'very close' to a deal
articles.summary: Vance says US and Iran are 'very close' to a deal The US and Iran are "very close" to a memorandum of understanding, Vice President JD Vance said, adding that this would extend the ceasefire by 60 days. He also indicated that the deal would reopen the Strait of Hormuz as well as begin talks on  limiting Tehran's nuclear program. "We're going back and forth on a couple of language points. We've made a lot of progress here," Vance said. "I can't guarantee that we're going to get there... but right now I feel pretty good about it."

articles.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Yield Rises After Lukewarm Auction

articles.title: US says its sanctioning entities, individuals linked to Iran's oil economy
articles.summary: US says its sanctioning entities, individuals linked to Iran's oil economy The Department of State said it was sanctioning several  entities, individuals "involved in the transportation of Iranian petroleum or petrochemical products". The department said that the move aims to "reduce revenue that the Iranian regime uses to attack its neighbors, support terrorism abroad, and oppress its own people".

articles.title: US energy executives warn of massive oil supply crunch
articles.summary: US energy executives warn of massive oil supply crunch Executives from the largest US energy companies warned on Thursday that oil prices are likely to spike this summer, as the buffers the US and other western countries used to mitigate the impact of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz run dry. “We're approaching unheard of inventory levels. I mean, really, really low levels. You can debate whether that's going to hit those really low levels in two weeks or three weeks. Once you get to that point, then you'll see [the] price shoot up,” Neil Chapman, a senior vice president at ExxonMobil, said

articles.title: Netanyahu aims to expand Israel's seizure of Gaza to 70 percent
articles.summary: Netanyahu aims to expand Israel's seizure of Gaza to 70 percent Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday signaled plans for Israel to control 70 percent of the Gaza Strip.  "We currently control 60 percent of the Gaza Strip, and my directives are to move toward controlling 70 percent," the Israeli premier said during a seminar in the Jordan Valley. Currently, Israel has expanded its seizure of Gaza to 60 percent of the territory, despite the ceasefire that envisaged a gradual Israeli withdrawal from all of Gaza.

articles.title: Israeli strike causes massive fire in Khan Younis
articles.summary: Israeli strike causes massive fire in Khan Younis Palestinian civil defence workers in Khan Younis are battling a massive fire following a new Israeli strike. The attack reportedly targeted a residential block containing displacement tents and commercial warehouses. Footage online shows structures and vehicles set ablaze, as rescue attempt to put the fire out.  Palestinian civil defence workers in Khan Younis are battling a massive warehouse fire with severely depleted resources following a new Israeli strike. pic.twitter.com/cwKsKQG2Ym — Middle East Eye (@MiddleEastEye) May 28, 2026

articles.title: Washington denies Iranian reports of shooting down US plane
articles.summary: Washington denies Iranian reports of shooting down US plane The US ⁠Central Command on Friday indicated that none of its aircrafts were shot down, following Iranian reports on downing a plane.  "No U.S. aircraft ​were shot ​down. ⁠All U.S. air assets are ​accounted for," Centcom said in a post ⁠on ​X. articles.title: Israeli strike kills one, injures several in Gaza City
articles.summary: Israeli strike kills one, injures several in Gaza City An Israeli strike on the Zeitoun neighborhood, Gaza City, killed one Palestinian and injured several others.  So far, the Palestinian Health Ministry in the territory reported over 922 deaths since the start of a ceasefire in October. Overall, at least 72,819 people have been killed in Israel's genocidal war on Gaza. Thousands more are missing and presumed dead under the rubble.

articles.title: Spanish police dismantle drone smuggling network moving drugs from Morocco to France
articles.summary: Spanish police have dismantled a cross border drug trafficking network that used large fixed wing drones to transport hashish and cocaine from northern Morocco into southern Spain before moving the shipments onward to France, authorities said. The operation, dubbed “Horus,” led to the arrest of eight suspects and the seizure of 40 kilograms of hashish, […] The post Spanish police dismantle drone smuggling network moving drugs from Morocco to France appeared first on HESPRESS English - Morocco News .

articles.title: Bessent says no sanctions relief before Hormuz reopens and uranium surrendered
articles.summary: Bessent says no sanctions relief before Hormuz reopens and uranium surrendered US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says Washington will not consider lifting sanctions on Iran until the Strait of Hormuz is reopened and Tehran agrees to hand over its stockpile of highly enriched uranium. “Nothing is going to be on the table until we see the Strait of Hormuz open, and the Iranians agree they have to turn over the highly enriched uranium,” Bessent said.
